decision_standard.primary_metric_implementation: compute_episode_metrics request-time Bt/Ut snapshot aggregator; provisional pending Garrido same-timestamp confirmation
decision_standard.superseded_metric_rule: Every ret_excel_visible_v1 OAT-ledger result is metric-development only and cannot establish H_PI, H_obs, a null, a positive or a ceiling without v2 rescore.
decision_standard.secondary_metric_governance: Cobb-Douglas 2024 and program_g.metrics_all are secondary construct-sensitivity analyses only and cannot promote, close or rescue a primary claim
decision_standard.resources: candidate totals of every physical resource and every action destination must be componentwise non-superior under the locked same-contract ledger
decision_standard.static_frontier: full-horizon same-contract open-loop schedules plus strongest classical and interpretable policies
decision_standard.feedback_certificate: modal sequence, calibration-only calendar and phase-only replacement must strictly underperform
decision_standard.paper3_authorization: learned Paper-2 value must first be confirmed

included: versioned Track A-C and Program D-K3 researcher extensions already implemented in the repository

theory-first embeddability tests for product mix, censored demand, admission, lateral transshipment, capacity reservation, multi-echelon information lag, finite storage, inspection, component-kit balancing, defect disposition, integrated recovery and alarm maintenance
searched_envelope.not_claimed: A universal boundary over arbitrary future researcher extensions is impossible because unconstrained extensions can embed a headroom-bearing POMDP.
searched_envelope.state_rule: Only versioned numerical contracts may be falsified. A mechanism that needs absent operational facts is blocked_domain_fact, not empirically falsified.
searched_envelope.terminal_boundary_rule: A terminal boundary cannot be inferred from local nulls, an invalidated stylized atlas or incomplete comparator families; each active family requires an explicit quantitative ceiling under the canonical metric and locked resource ledger.

approaches.hypothesis: Persistent mission or climate mix, observed before a product-specific batch commitment, reverses which ration type should use scarce shared line capacity.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Two or more nonfungible ration types sharing Op5-Op7 capacity, product-specific BOMs, batch/setup commitments and approved substitutions.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose the product of each 48-hour 5000-ration batch; later only if validated, allocate integer batches.
approaches.observations: Product inventory/pipeline/backlog plus a timestamped operational mission or climate signal.
approaches.information_lead: Must precede the last irrevocable product-mix commitment by at least its production and pipeline lead.
approaches.resource_constraint: Raw-material units by ingredient, shared line hours, setup hours/lost yield, batch count, Op8 vehicle-hours, weight and volume.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Different products are optimal in material state shares as the latent product-demand regime and product inventory vector change.

h_pi_estimate.warning: No multi-product contract has been simulated; the current single homogeneous product has exact zero product-choice liveness.

approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis documents 21 products but specifies only one product's BOM and no product demand, substitution, setup or signal data; an exact small-state DP also blocks a neural-increment claim by policy-class containment.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: single homogeneous thesis product or fully fungible equal-BOM zero-setup labels

approaches.null_regime: Identical BOM/rate, zero setup, complete substitution and label-independent eligibility; every action trajectory must hash identically.
evidence_required_to_change_state: approved substitution matrix and ReT fulfillment semantics
pre-commitment signal timestamps and plausible forecast envelope
explicit Garrido authorization of the numerical extension
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Which of the 21 ration types share Op5-Op7, what are their BOMs, line rates, batch/changeover/minimum-run rules, mission and climate substitution matrix, product-demand shares and timestamps, and which operational signal is available before the mix is committed?

approaches.hypothesis: A link-specific signal before an irrevocable shared-lift reservation lets the operator reserve scarce Op10/Op12 capacity for the route whose future lead-time regime makes it most valuable.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Finite common transport pool, L-step booking lead, minimum dwell/non-free cancellation, link-specific persistent degradation and advance signal.
approaches.decision_rights: Reserve future capacity for Op10 or Op12 under a componentwise fleet ledger.
approaches.observations: Inventory, pipeline, backlog and an operational route/tempo signal available before booking.
approaches.information_lead: At least the booking activation lead; post-booking warnings are not actionable.
approaches.resource_constraint: Booked slots, vehicle-hours, departures, payload, cancellations, unused slots and reallocations.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Marginal slot value M10 exceeds M12 in some material states and reverses in others.

h_pi_estimate.warning: Program I is not a global reservation ceiling.

approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis fixes 24-hour lead times and takes vehicle availability and route planning for granted. Program M therefore tests a disclosed synthetic protected-lift extension whose numerical hazard, signal, slot and bypass assumptions are not thesis estimates. Its binary A/B structure may be solved by an interpretable signal or belief rule, so observable headroom would not imply learned incremental value.

program_m_extension.claim_scope: Conditional adaptive value in a disclosed synthetic multimodal contested-access extension only; not numerical reproduction of the thesis fleet and not learned value.

approaches.null_regime: Native fixed 24-hour lead, nonbinding capacity, no booking lead/free cancellation, or identical nonpersistent route regimes.
evidence_required_to_change_state: immutable executable producer and whole-session watcher
H_PI gate on the frozen connected-region rule
later independent face validation before an MFSC-domain claim
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Are Op10 and Op12 served by one finite shared fleet that must be reserved in advance; if so, what are its capacity, vehicle-hours, booking lead, dwell/cancellation/reassignment rules, link-specific degraded lead times and pre-booking signal characteristics?

approaches.hypothesis: When stockouts censor true theatre demand, a scarce early field report improves the belief used for a later binding replenishment or production commitment.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Latent persistent tempo, censored issues, evaluator-sealed true demand and a resource-limited report acquired before commitment.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose report acquisition and replenishment/production action.
approaches.observations: Issued quantity, stockout indicator, inventory/pipeline, posterior and optional report; never true censored demand.
approaches.information_lead: Report must arrive before the physical order/production deadline.
approaches.resource_constraint: Named staff-hours, communication slots or verified reports plus existing inventory/capacity budgets.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Belief-dependent replenishment or reservation changes with posterior tempo and inventory position.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The current MFSC records requested orders; without true censoring and a real active-report resource this is the closed K2/K3 replenishment family.
approaches.null_regime: Full demand observation, uninformative report, no stockout censoring or zero physical lead.
approaches.exact_garrido_question: During a CSSU/theatre stockout, is complete requested quantity recorded or only issued quantity; can a report be requested before commitment, what does it reveal, how early, and at what operational resource cost?

approaches.hypothesis: Delayed, resource-consuming lateral transfers can exploit observable donor/recipient imbalances after central dispatch is committed.
approaches.physical_mechanism: CSSU-to-CSSU lane with conserved in-transit inventory and finite lift.
approaches.observations: Local inventory/backlog/pipeline and persistent local demand context.
approaches.information_lead: Imbalance must be known while a lateral arrival can still change affected order ReT.
approaches.resource_constraint: Transfer load, trips, vehicle-hours and inventory in transit.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Donor and recipient identities reverse across states.
comparator_family: joint central plus lateral full-horizon schedules
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: No lateral lane is thesis-native and direct central resupply weakly dominates when it is no slower, consumes the same lift and SB stock exists.
approaches.null_regime: Identical theatre proportions and synchronized demand regimes.
evidence_required_to_change_state: existence and authority of lateral resupply
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Do CSSUs laterally resupply one another; if so, is that route faster than SB resupply, what vehicle/load/travel/return/risk rules apply, and is its lift distinct from SB-to-CSSU transport?

approaches.hypothesis: Delayed CSSU reports create a new adaptive allocation problem.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Information lag with unchanged Program G/H physics and action rights.
approaches.decision_rights: No genuinely new authority as stated.
approaches.observations: A strict subset of the current observation history.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: Removing information cannot outperform the optimal current-information policy, but that policy has not been bounded under ret_excel_request_snapshot_v2. The historical Program-H ceiling uses a non-governing full-ledger adapter and the later visible-v1 repair is quarantined.
evidence_required_to_change_state: authorized causally live Program-H route adapter
request-snapshot-v2 replay of all 81 four-week sequences
finite-horizon observation-history or belief-policy bound if v2 PI remains material
validated report latency and persistent leading signal
approaches.exact_garrido_question: What inventory/backlog/consumption/convoy reports move from each CSSU to SB, with what latency/error, which decisions are central versus local, and are 24-hour central shipments irrevocable?

approaches.hypothesis: Observed mission deadlines make state-dependent admission and priority outperform a fixed service doctrine.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Physical order expiry/abandonment plus explicit admission authority and released service capacity.
approaches.observations: Queue, workload, observed class/deadline and service state.
approaches.resource_constraint: daily production/dispatch capacity; every rejection counted as lost
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: priority/admission changes with deadline, reward and service time.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis backlogs orders rather than establishing physical abandonment; rejection is prohibited shed-to-win under the lost-order guardrail.
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Does an unfilled ration order become useless after a mission/deployment window, is it cancelled and logged as lost, can logistics reject/defer/reroute it, and what observed deadline classes and frequencies apply?

approaches.hypothesis: A leading condition alarm lets one finite crew protect a station whose failure cannot be covered by inventory.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Persistent degradation, noisy condition and one-week failure forecast, one shared 24-hour crew and no finished-goods cover in the stylized weekly model.
approaches.observations: Condition and noisy one-week threat forecast.
approaches.information_lead: one week in the implemented screen
approaches.resource_constraint: one equal 24-hour crew allocation per decision epoch

h_pi_estimate.evidence_metric: ret_excel_full_ledger historical; central v2 development point only

approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The historical 0.0001338 screen is non-governing. The complete central-cell 3^8 v2 point diagnostic is 0.000182735 but uses 12 burned tapes, one of 108 cells, a historical resource slice and no confidence ceiling or worst-node endpoint. Changing only signal accuracy or severity remains a forbidden reparameterization.

current_physics_ceiling.relation: The central point is 54.7 times below the practical gate but does not upper-bound the other 107 cells or establish H_obs.

approaches.null_regime: No degradation/failure effect or nonbinding crew.
evidence_required_to_change_state: complete 6,561-schedule eight-week request-snapshot-v2 frontier across the frozen 108-cell design with crew and guardrail ledgers
materially different domain-validated maintenance authority or downstream consequence, not severity/signal scaling
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Is a condition signal available before an actual Op5-Op7 breakdown and can a finite shared crew prevent/shorten it; what are signal errors/lead, preventive efficacy/duration, repair duration and finished-stock cover?

approaches.hypothesis: When one R21/R3 event disables several operations concurrently, fewer cross-trained Maintenance Battalion teams than disabled sites force serial recovery and observable damage estimates reverse the best next repair.
approaches.physical_mechanism: A finite mobile restoration-team pool with travel, activation, nonzero repair work and mutually exclusive assignment across concurrently disabled operations.
approaches.decision_rights: Dispatch or reassign a restoration team to one disabled operation; optionally preempt only if the real rule permits it.
approaches.observations: Timestamped damage assessment, operation state, backlog/WIP and team location available before dispatch; no future repair duration or risk tape.
approaches.information_lead: damage assessment must arrive before an irrevocable travel/repair commitment
approaches.resource_constraint: team-hours, travel-hours, activation, skills and simultaneous-use exclusion; one team cannot repair two sites concurrently
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: the operation with greatest marginal canonical ReT recovery changes with damage duration, WIP/backlog, downstream cover and team location.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis supplies simultaneous outage/recovery distributions and names MaB but does not supply team count, serialized work, travel, preemption or pre-dispatch damage information. A historical FALSIFIED label relied on an unproved restoration-order-invariance assertion and is not a machine ceiling.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: independent parallel recovery clocks and no shared-team dispatch action

approaches.null_regime: Thesis-default independent parallel recovery with no scarce shared team.
evidence_required_to_change_state: Maintenance Battalion team count and skill compatibility
approaches.exact_garrido_question: After an R21 or R3 event, does the Maintenance Battalion have fewer cross-trained recovery teams than disabled sites, forcing repairs to serialize; if yes, what are team count, bases/travel/activation, operation-specific repair distributions, preemption, damage-assessment lead/error, and whether 120/672 hours is work content or autonomous downtime?

approaches.hypothesis: Nonfungible finite space with slow relocation forces state-dependent allocation among products or locations.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Finite location/product-specific storage, overflow consequence and relocation/reconfiguration lead.
approaches.observations: Inventory, pipeline, forecast and space commitments.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: space priority reverses across product/location regimes.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis assumes unlimited storage; homogeneous instantaneously fungible space reduces to the already-tested aggregate buffer-size family.
approaches.null_regime: Capacity at or above maximum inventory, or fully fungible zero-lead reconfiguration.
approaches.exact_garrido_question: What are usable capacities at WDC, assembly, SB and CSSUs, which space is dedicated/fungible, what happens on overflow, and what resource/lead is required to relocate or reconfigure stock?

approaches.hypothesis: A scarce Op7 inspection effort trades current throughput for qualified output under a persistent, signaled lot-quality regime.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Variable sampling/inspection time, imperfect sensitivity/specificity, CRN-coupled exogenous per-unit/lot defect draws, escaped-defect consequences and Op6 rework.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose inspection intensity or allocate inspectors.
approaches.observations: Quality signal, backlog, inventory and process state.
approaches.information_lead: quality indicators available before batch release
approaches.resource_constraint: inspection labor/minutes, line hours, rework and qualified output
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Simplified screen only: with normalized throughput, no false positives, immediate unit-yield recovery and qualified output (1-delta)(1-p+p*s), throughput penalty delta must satisfy T(.03)<=delta<T(.08), T(p)=p(s_H-s_L)/(1-p+p*s_H); with extreme sensitivities 0 and 1 this is 0.03<=delta<0.08. This is not a general inspection theorem, and a persistent pre-action quality state is still absent.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: R14 supplies fixed 3%/8% Binomial defects and says a detected item returns to Op6; it does not establish 100% detection. The current DES treats every generated defect as detected. Neither source supplies variable inspection, a persistent quality regime, a leading signal or field-escape endpoint. If detection controls the R14 metric gate, inspect-less can manufacture ReT by concealing risk.
approaches.null_regime: The current kernel has no inspection action and therefore exact zero incremental headroom. An introduced action is exactly transition-dead only when detection, qualification, rework/escape and throughput responses are all effort-invariant. Constant-p/no-quality-signal blocks the proposed signal-responsive mechanism but does not rule out backlog/cover feedback; zero escape consequence may make intensive inspection dominated without making the action transition-dead.
evidence_required_to_change_state: persistent lot-quality state and pre-action signal
latent defective = inspected TP + inspected FN + uninspected defective
latent conforming = inspected TN + inspected FP + uninspected conforming
escaped defective = inspected FN + uninspected defective
inspected TP + inspected FP = reworked + scrapped + held/queued
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Is Op7 100% inspection or sampling; may intensity vary; what inspector minutes, sensitivity/specificity/false rejects, lot persistence and pre-action signals, escaped-defect consequence, rework yield/time and AQL apply?

approaches.hypothesis: Urgency or route information known before dispatch reverses which scarce transport mode should serve Op10/Op12 while mode commitment removes future fleet-hours.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Land, air or water modes with different payload, lead, cost/risk exposure, turnaround and persistent fleet commitment.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose mode and destination for an irrevocable dispatch.
approaches.observations: Inventory/backlog, mission urgency, mode availability and nonprivileged route condition.
approaches.resource_constraint: mode-specific vehicle/aircraft hours, payload, trips, turnaround and simultaneous-use exclusion
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: fast/safer scarce mode is optimal for urgent or threatened orders while high-capacity slower mode is optimal otherwise.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis names land/sea/water/air choices but supplies no mode-specific numerical physics and takes vehicle availability/routes for granted; the repo collapses arcs to 24 h.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: all modes collapsed to the same 24-hour unlimited-availability arc

approaches.null_regime: Modes have identical lead, payload, exposure and nonbinding availability.
approaches.exact_garrido_question: For Op10/Op12 delivery, which land, air or water modes are available; what are their payload, fleet count, one-way/turnaround time, mission eligibility and R22 exposure; what is known before dispatch; and does committing one mode remove that asset from later epochs?

approaches.hypothesis: A route-specific warning available before an irrevocable finite-fleet dispatch reverses which of two routes should carry the same shipment.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Two routes to one downstream demand with different transit, persistent R22 exposure and a single vehicle committed through its return leg.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose HOLD, primary route or alternate route at dispatch.
approaches.observations: Inventory, backlog, vehicle state and noisy route-specific warning available before departure.
approaches.information_lead: The warning must precede departure and remain informative over the committed transit window.
approaches.resource_constraint: Departures, payload, outbound and return vehicle-hours, fleet availability and downstream inventory are componentwise matched.
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: The primary route ranks first when healthy; a slower alternate ranks first only under route-specific persistent degradation.

h_pi_estimate.warning: The v1 full-DES quantity labelled H_PI was a myopic true-state rule and is not an oracle or upper bound.

h_obs_estimate.resource_warning: Both positive-mean cells used more departures than the small comparator set.

approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis takes vehicle availability and route planning as given. The alternate route, 36-hour legs, degradation penalty/persistence and warning accuracy are researcher-introduced and unvalidated. The concurrent screen also lacked a true PI bound, full open-loop frontier, resource equality and trajectory audit.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: thesis-native and pre-Program-L DES has no alternate-route decision

approaches.null_regime: Only one route, common-mode degradation, identical route physics, nonbinding fleet, or a signal revealed after departure.
evidence_required_to_change_state: Garrido face validation of the alternate route and finite shared fleet
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Does the MFSC operator choose among at least two routes from Op8 toward the same downstream demand using one finite shared fleet; if so, what are each route's payload, outbound/return times, R22 exposure, commitment and reassignment rules, degradation persistence and warning available before dispatch, and are 36 h each way, +24 h degraded, persistence 0.85, prevalence 0.25 and signal accuracy 0.85 plausible?

approaches.hypothesis: One real resource with travel, activation and dwell must be committed mutually exclusively among plant, route and theatre recovery, creating persistent cross-stage ranking reversals.
approaches.physical_mechanism: A single skill/fleet/team pool couples otherwise separable recovery actions intertemporally.
approaches.decision_rights: Allocate the resource among manufacturing, LOC and mission response.
approaches.observations: Nonprivileged disruption, condition, backlog and location state.
approaches.information_lead: enough to act before the affected recovery/service window closes
approaches.resource_constraint: team-hours, skills, travel, activation, dwell and simultaneous-use exclusion
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: target with highest marginal canonical ReT recovery changes across concurrent disruptions.

h_pi_estimate.warning: The W12/W16 reduced-horizon semantic certifications do not compute or bound the full 24-week resource-restricted H_PI.

approaches.main_threat_to_validity: Separate resources factorize into closed families; a generic token pool reduces to Program F. The frozen resource contract matches one 4032-hour team-week and identical reserve access, while treating reserve issue/replenishment/request counts as conserved endogenous outcomes. The real cross-stage team identity and any separately finite reserve transport/handling resource remain unvalidated by Garrido. Every completed comparator screen used the superseded visible-v1 ledger and is now metric-development only; v2 H_PI and H_obs are uncomputed.

adversarial_hold.release_rule: All 507-calendar, <=3-switch, <=4 preflight and partial producer scores used ret_excel_visible_v1 and are quarantined. The watched <=4 producer was deliberately terminated after 5/60 tapes when the metric defect was confirmed. Restart from an immutable v2 commit, recalibrate from the first tape, rebuild the open-loop frontier and resource-restricted PI, and retain the full custody/deep-replay gates.

reference_calendar_screen.verdict: selected reference precluded for resource-relaxed W24 only; family remains unresolved

switch_complexity_screen.verdict: calibration comparator boundary active; authorize only a separately frozen <=4-switch comparator gate

current_physics_ceiling.claim_boundary: No family ceiling and no terminal boundary certificate.

approaches.null_regime: Separate nontransferable resources or no target-dependent causal effect.
evidence_required_to_change_state: immutable request-snapshot-v2 restart of comparator calibration
complete open-loop frontier or certified approximation bound under v2
Garrido validation of request-snapshot same-time semantics and reserve transport/handling semantics
real fungible shared-resource identity and operational constraints
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Is there one truly fungible resource allocated mutually exclusively among plant repair, LOC/vehicle recovery and theatre response; where is it based, what skills/travel/activation/dwell apply, and how does one unit causally change recovery time at each target? For each R24 reserve issue and Op9 order-up-to replenishment, is transport/handling/order processing non-scarce or does it consume a separately finite departure, vehicle-hour, crew-hour, fuel/budget or slot shared with Op10/Op12 or M/T/R; is 5000 units a per-event, per-week or per-episode limit; and how is the prepositioned 10000-unit stock costed?

approaches.hypothesis: A mission plan observed before departure reverses which cohort should receive a fixed total carried-ration loadout when resupply reliability and mission duration differ persistently.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Irreversible pre-departure allocation of personal carried inventory among mission cohorts, with cohort-specific consumption and delayed return or resupply.
approaches.decision_rights: Allocate a fixed total number and mass of ration packs among cohorts before deployment.
approaches.observations: Timestamped mission duration, planned resupply interval and route/accessibility class available before departure.
approaches.information_lead: mission and resupply plan must arrive before personal loadout becomes cohort-specific and non-reallocatable
approaches.resource_constraint: total packs, kilograms, cohort carry limits, CSSU stock deductions and explicit unused-pack return/transfer ledger
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: the cohort with the greatest marginal canonical benefit changes with predicted mission duration, resupply interruption and current carried inventory.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis motivates carried rations and mission-dependent loadout, but the current DES ends at theatre delivery and has no personal-carry state, cohort demand ledger, loadout authority, pack/mass constraint or return rule.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: current DES has no mission-cohort carried-inventory state or loadout action

approaches.null_regime: Identical mission distributions, fully reliable resupply, shuffled or post-departure signals, or a nonbinding carry limit.
evidence_required_to_change_state: sealed order semantics for carried-stock consumption
approaches.exact_garrido_question: Before deployment, may logistics allocate a fixed total ration loadout among mission cohorts using observed mission-duration and resupply plans; what are the pack/mass limits, decision authority, signal timestamps and accuracy, demand-ledger semantics, and return/transfer rules?

approaches.hypothesis: With one frozen finished-ration type and no output-product choice/substitution, named complementary-input R13 delays change the kit-limiting component and an observed component pipeline can reverse which equal-payload Op4 load or expedition should be committed.
approaches.physical_mechanism: Component-specific rm1...rm12 inventory and pipeline, BOM consumption, finite mixed-load Op4 lift and optional conserved expedition resource.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose component composition/priority of the next capacity-limited Op4 shipment or expend a matched expedition token.
approaches.observations: Component on-hand and pipeline by identity plus nonprivileged supplier-delay status available before load commitment.
approaches.information_lead: must precede Op4 dispatch by its loading and 24-hour transit lead
approaches.resource_constraint: component units, payload weight/volume, departures, vehicle-hours, minimum lots and expedition tokens
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: For K(x,p)=min_i floor((x_i+p_i)/b_i), equal-resource loads a,b are live only if K(x,p+y_a) differs from K(x,p+y_b); the limiting component must change over material observable states.
comparator_family: all full-horizon component-load schedules or certified MILP bound
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: The thesis names 12 raw materials but the DES aggregates them, R13 is a common Op2 delay, and Op4 has neither component-load authority nor finite lift. If delays are common, kits fixed-proportion, lift nonbinding or identities unobserved, one doctrine dominates.
approaches.null_regime: Scalar common delay, proportional component inventories, fixed kit loads or nonbinding lift gives H_PI=H_obs=0. If component identity exists but is unavailable before dispatch, H_obs=0 while H_PI may remain positive and must not be called zero.
evidence_required_to_change_state: finite mixed-load Op4 authority and return cycle
approaches.exact_garrido_question: In R13, do delayed deliveries identify named rm1...rm12 components, and may WDC choose the component composition or priority of a capacity-limited Op4 shipment; what BOM, inventory/pipeline visibility, payload, return, minimum-lot, expedition and substitution rules apply?

approaches.hypothesis: After an observed R14 detection, state-dependent line and raw-material shadow values can reverse whether a held lot should be reworked or scrapped and replaced.
approaches.physical_mechanism: A discretionary, quality-constrained lot disposition with distinct rework duration/yield/capacity and replacement material/lead.
approaches.decision_rights: Choose hold/rework versus scrap-and-replace after defect quantity is known; shipping known nonconforming output is prohibited.
approaches.observations: Defect quantity, rework queue, raw-material cover, backlog and line availability known before disposition.
approaches.information_lead: after Op7 detection but before Op6 rework or replacement commitment
approaches.resource_constraint: line-hours, rework capacity, replacement raw materials, qualified output, disposal and persistent R14 attribution
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: Reduced shadow-cost screen only: D_reduced(s)=lambda_C(s)(h_r/y_r-h_n)-lambda_R(s)b, with lambda_C in canonical-value/line-hour, lambda_R in canonical-value/material-unit, h_r rework hours, y_r rework yield, h_n replacement-production hours and b replacement material units. A sign change is necessary only inside that reduced model, which omits lead, hold, disposal, repeat-defect and deadline effects; a licensed contract needs D_total(s). Under current same-rate unit-yield physics, rework weakly dominates discard on qualified service/material use.
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: Return to Op6 is thesis-mandated and constructor mode is not an adaptive right. Discard can also launder the R14 Excel risk label unless attribution follows replacement.
approaches.null_regime: Mandatory unit-yield rework at the same effective line rate with no extra consequence.
approaches.exact_garrido_question: When Op7 detects R14 nonconforming rations, is return to Op6 mandatory or may the operator choose hold/rework versus scrap-and-replace; what timing, duration, capacity, yield, material, lead, release, disposal, quality and R14-attribution rules apply?

approaches.hypothesis: Changing requested demand creates adaptive value.
approaches.resource_constraint: original demand remains in sealed evaluator ledger
approaches.expected_ranking_reversal: only potentially valid for approved product alternatives
approaches.main_threat_to_validity: Troop ration quantity is not a controllable demand variable; deletion, deferral or denominator rewriting is shed-to-win.

current_physics_ceiling.condition: original order not retained or lost orders increase

approaches.exact_garrido_question: May commanders satisfy a requisition with another ration type without violating nutrition, climate, mission or contract requirements, and when is that substitution decision made and logged?

registry_summary.mtr_adversarial_hold: Every completed M/T/R switch frontier used the quarantined visible-v1 metric. The 60-tape VPS producer was stopped after 5/60 and yielded no result; six orphan workers exposed a parent-PID-only watcher defect and were terminated. A v2 restart requires a new immutable commit and whole-session watcher. W24, H_PI, an observable-policy claim and all learner work remain unauthorized.
formally_dominated_historical_contracts: K3_budgeted_replenishment under frozen ret_excel_full_ledger_order; exact H_PI=H_obs=0 over all 6,371 exact-budget trajectories and all 5,758,374 non-superior-budget grid calendars, with under-spending pairs not mislabeled equal-resource; visible-v1 not unconditionally certified
